commands.review_summary:
The PR strips dangling high surrogates after three Browser plugin text truncation slices and adds focused tests for the truncation expression.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +48. Total +50 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection and a focused Node expression probe show current main can leave a trailing high surrogate when an emoji straddles the existing UTF-16 code-unit slice boundary.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed because there are no actionable review findings and the remaining action is ordinary maintainer approval or merge handling.

target.title: fix(browser): truncate element text on code-point boundaries to avoid lone surrogates
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Three browser snapshot/text-extraction paths truncate element text content with
raw `.slice(0, N)`. When an emoji or other astral character straddles the cut
point, the raw slice keeps only the high-surrogate half, producing a lone
`\uD83D` — malformed UTF-16.

Affected paths:
- `extensions/browser/src/browser/cdp.ts:623`: `findCursorInteractiveElements`
  truncates `textContent` at 100 code units
- `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.snapshot.ts:80`:
  `collectSnapshotUrls` truncates anchor label at 120 code units
- `extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.snapshot.ts:81`:
  `collectChromeMcpSnapshotUrls` truncates anchor label at 120 code units

## Why This Change Was Made

Since the affected code runs in the browser page context (CDP `Runtime.evaluate`
/ Playwright `page.evaluate`), the SDK's `truncateUtf16Safe` is not importable.
Instead, `.replace(/[\uD800-\uDBFF]$/, "")` is appended after each `.slice()`
to strip any dangling high surrogate at the cut boundary. This preserves the
original UTF-16 code-unit cap while avoiding broken surrogate pairs.

## User Impact

No user-facing behavior change. Element text sent to the LLM now contains valid
UTF-16 regardless of where emoji fall in the truncated string. The code-unit
caps (100 / 120) remain unchanged.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm test extensions/browser` — 59 tests passed

### Real behavior proof

The proof calls `snapshotRoleViaCdp` — an exported OpenClaw browser module
function that internally calls `findCursorInteractiveElements` (cdp.ts:580)
and `buildCdpRoleSnapshot`, which exercise the patched truncation expression
at cdp.ts:623 — against a real Chrome page with emoji at the truncation
boundary.

```
$ node --import tsx proof-role-snapshot.mts

- RootWebArea
  - paragraph
    - StaticText "xxx...😀tail"                  ← emoji at boundary, clean
  - link "xxx...🎉link text" [ref=e1]            ← emoji at boundary, clean
    - StaticText "xxx..."

Stats: {"lines":15,"chars":918,"refs":2,"interactive":2}
Dangling surrogates in snapshot: false        ✅
```

This output was produced by running `extensions/browser/src/browser/cdp.js`
through the actual OpenClaw browser module call chain (`snapshotRoleViaCdp` →
`buildCdpRoleSnapshot` → `findCursorInteractiveElements`). All text in the
snapshot is free of dangling surrogates.

The same `.replace()` is applied identically in `pw-tools-core.snapshot.ts:80`
and `routes/agent.snapshot.ts:81`.

## Tests

`extensions/browser/src/browser/browser-text-truncation.test.ts` — 6 new tests
covering ASCII pass-through, long ASCII truncation, dangling-surrogate removal,
multiple emoji, code-unit cap preservation, and complete-pair pass-through.
target.author: solodmd

commands.author: solodmd

target.title: fix(agents): bound body-less MCP HTTP text responses
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`ensureGlobalFetchResponse` in the MCP HTTP fetch wrapper previously read body-less `Response`-like objects via `await response.text()`. That compatibility path exists so the MCP SDK can parse OAuth error text from foreign response implementations, but a body-less response with no reliable size signal can otherwise force OpenClaw to allocate an unbounded string.

This PR makes that body-less fallback policy explicit: text-only foreign responses are preserved only when they advertise a safe `Content-Length`; missing, invalid, or oversized lengths fail closed before calling `text()`.

## Changes

- `src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.ts`
  - Adds `MCP_HTTP_MAX_TEXT_RESPONSE_BYTES = 1 * 1024 * 1024`.
  - Parses `Content-Length` for body-less foreign responses.
  - Returns `new Response(null, init)` without calling `response.text()` when `Content-Length` is missing, invalid, negative, or over the cap.
  - Preserves small body-less OAuth error text only when the declared length is within the cap.
  - Keeps a post-read UTF-8 byte-length check as defense in depth if the declared length lies.
- `src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.test.ts`
  - Keeps MCP SDK OAuth parsing coverage for small body-less error text with safe `Content-Length`.
  - Adds coverage that declared-oversized body-less responses do not call `text()`.
  - Adds coverage that no-`Content-Length` body-less responses do not call `text()`.
  - Adds coverage that declared-small but actually oversized text is dropped after the post-read UTF-8 byte check.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: A body-less MCP HTTP foreign response with missing or oversized `Content-Length` must not call `text()` at all; the wrapper should preserve status/headers and return `body=null`.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Node v22 workspace, real `http.createServer`, production `buildMcpHttpFetch`, and a custom FetchLike adapter that converts the real HTTP response into the MCP body-less foreign-response shape.
- **Exact steps**: A local HTTP server returned a 1 MiB + 1 byte body. The proof called production `buildMcpHttpFetch` for both `/declared-large` and `/no-length`; the adapter counted calls to the foreign response `text()` method.
- **Observed result after fix**: both responses returned status 400 with `body=null`, and `textCalls=0`.
- **What was not tested**: A live external MCP OAuth server was not hit; this proof uses a local loopback server plus the same production MCP fetch wrapper and body-less foreign-response contract.

Loopback proof output:

```json
{"declared":{"status":400,"body":null},"unbounded":{"status":400,"body":null},"textCalls":0,"declaredTextCalls":0}
```

## Compatibility / Policy Note

This PR intentionally changes no-`Content-Length` body-less text-only MCP OAuth errors from readable text to `body=null`. That is the fail-closed option from #97521. Maintainer/security review still needs to accept that policy before merge.

For declared-small body-less responses, the only available API is `text()`, so the implementation trusts `Content-Length` before reading and then verifies the returned UTF-8 byte length. The new regression test covers a lying response that declares `Content-Length: 64` but returns more than 1 MiB; the wrapper drops it after the post-read check. Fully avoiding allocation for that lying declared-small shape would require failing closed for all body-less text responses, including the current OAuth compatibility case.

## Evidence

Focused Vitest after the latest update:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/mcp-http-fetch.test.ts --run

Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
```

Whitespace/check sanity:

```
$ git diff --check
# exit 0
```

Earlier type checks on this PR:

```
$ pnpm tsgo:core      # exit 0
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test # exit 0
```
